defs: To roar; to bellow; to snort; to snore loudly.
A bellowing; a shouting; noise; clamor; uproar; disturbance; tumult.
quotes: This new book the whole world makes such a rout about.
“My child, it is not well,” I said, “Among the graves to shout; To laugh and play among the dead, And make this noisy rout.”

defs: To scoop out with a gouge or other tool; to furrow.
To search or root in the ground, as a swine.

records.ety: [OF. route, LL. rupta, properly, a breaking, fr. L. ruptus, p. p. of rumpere to break. See Rupture, reave, and cf. Rote repetition of forms, Route. In some senses this word has been confused with rout a bellowing, an uproar.]

defs: A troop; a throng; a company; an assembly; especially, a traveling company or throng.
quotes: And ever he rode the hinderest of the route.
A rout of people there assembled were.

defs: A disorderly and tumultuous crowd; a mob; hence, the rabble; the herd of common people.
quotes: the endless routs of wretched thralls.
The ringleader and head of all this rout.
Nor do I name of men the common rout.

defs: The state of being disorganized and thrown into confusion; -- said especially of an army defeated, broken in pieces, and put to flight in disorder or panic; also, the act of defeating and breaking up an army; as, the rout of the enemy was complete.
quotes: thy army . . . Dispersed in rout, betook them all to fly.
To these giad conquest, murderous rout to those.

defs: A disturbance of the peace by persons assembled together with intent to do a thing which, if executed, would make them rioters, and actually making a motion toward the executing thereof.
A fashionable assembly, or large evening party.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Routed; p. pr. & vb. n. Routing.]

defs: To break the ranks of, as troops, and put them to flight in disorder; to put to rout.
quotes: That party . . . that charged the Scots, so totally routed and defeated their whole army, that they fied.

defs: To assemble in a crowd, whether orderly or disorderly; to collect in company.
quotes: In all that land no Christian[s] durste route.
